idp.title: Identity providers

idp.name: Name

howto.title: Configure Google SSO — steps
howto.step_console: Open the {link} and select your Google Cloud project.
howto.step_create: Create an OAuth 2.0 client of type {type} (see the {link}).
howto.step_redirect: Add the authorized redirect URI below in your OAuth client settings.
howto.step_copy: Copy the Client ID and Client secret generated by Google.
howto.step_paste: Paste them in the form below, then save.
howto.step_enable: Check "SSO enabled" to show the Google sign-in button on the login page.

idp.redirect_uri_hint: Paste this URI as-is in Google Cloud Console → OAuth client → Authorized redirect URIs.
idp.copy_failed: Copy failed — select the URI manually.
idp.section_advanced_note: Default Google values — only change if you know what you are doing. See the
idp.client_secret_keep: Leave empty to keep the existing secret.

tooltip.name: Display name of the provider (e.g. Google). Used on the SSO sign-in button.
tooltip.issuer: OIDC issuer URL. For Google: https://accounts.google.com
tooltip.client_id: OAuth client ID created in Google Cloud Console.
tooltip.client_secret: OAuth client secret. Never share it publicly.
tooltip.jwks_uri: URL of Google's public JWT keys for token validation.
tooltip.scopes: OIDC scopes requested from Google (openid, profile, email).
tooltip.default_profile: Kore profile assigned to new users auto-created (JIT) on first SSO login.
tooltip.enabled: Enables or disables the SSO sign-in button on the login page.

hint.client_id: Format: xxxxxx.apps.googleusercontent.com — found in Google Cloud Console → Credentials.
hint.client_secret: Generated once when creating the OAuth client. Store it securely.
hint.default_profile: Collaborator suits most cases. Administrator is for management accounts only.
hint.enabled: Test sign-in from the login page after enabling.

budget.title: Budgets
budget.subtitle: UO budget tracking per application — fed by timesheets (CRA), used to quote TMA work.
budget.empty_desc: Budgets are created per application. They track planned vs consumed days, UO and amount.
budget.manager_section_help: Add an estimate or quote for a TMA request on this application.
budget.form_demand_help: Select the request to quote for this application.
budget.form_demand_empty: No TMA requests for this application
budget.tooltip_demand: Pick the TMA request to quote (linked to this application).
budget.tooltip_effort_days: Estimated effort in days. Use 0.5 increments (e.g. 1.5).
budget.tooltip_effort_uo: Estimated effort in UO. Use 0.5 increments (e.g. 2.0).
budget.tooltip_amount_eur: Quote amount in euros (e.g. 1250.00). Value must be ≥ 0.
budget.type_defaut_help: Reference budget required to create a TMA request on this application.
budget.integration_cra_desc: Days logged on this application feed budget consumption.
budget.integration_budget_desc: Days, UO and amount envelope — consumed vs planned.
budget.integration_tma_desc: Estimates and quotes linked to maintenance requests.

requests.form_chef_gate: Submit for business owner approval before processing
requests.attachments_hint: PDF, images, documents — 10 MB max per file

demandes.triage_subtitle: Pick the channel that fits your need
demandes.triage_support_desc: Question, access or light incident — quick response
demandes.triage_maintenance_desc: Infra, fixes or deployment — tracked in days
demandes.triage_empty: No request channel is available for your profile.

tma.title: What is this module for?
items: Handle application changes and incidents requiring analysis, budget or release.
BUILD team: application owner, developers, optional user lead gate.
On resolve: proposes one day (8 h) on the assignee timesheet.

support.title: What is this module for?
items: Helpdesk: user questions, access, light incidents without full TMA cycle.
DESK team: Support profile, threaded replies, no quote or analysis dossier.
On resolve: proposes one hour on the timesheet.

maintenance.title: What is this module for?
items: Operations and infra work without TMA overhead (fixes, deployments, ops).
RUN team: tracked in consumed days, no mandatory analysis artefacts.
On complete: proposes days × 8 h on the timesheet.

demandes.title: Configure request channels
items: ESN: enable TMA + User support for maintenance + helpdesk contracts.
Structured IT dept: add Operations & work for the RUN team.
Small team: TMA alone is often enough — disable other channels.

identity.title: Google SSO sign-in
items: Allows team members to sign in with their Google account (Gmail or Workspace).
Prerequisite: a Google Cloud project with an OAuth 2.0 Web application client.
Configure the Kore redirect URI in Google, then enter Client ID and secret here.
Password login remains available in parallel (dual-mode).
New SSO users receive the default profile configured below.

cra.title: What is this module for?
items: Enter and track monthly activity reports per mission.
Submit → manager validation workflow, with commercial info for client PDF.
Access planning and Gantt views to monitor workload and open timesheets.

conges.title: What is this module for?
items: Submit and track leave requests (paid leave, RTT, sick leave…).
View balances and history of approved absences.
Managers approve requests from the Validation tab.

budget.title: What is this module for?
items: Track work-unit budgets: planned vs consumed days per mission.
View consumption rate and overrun alerts.
Open a budget to detail lines and adjust forecasts.

invoicing.title: What is this module for?
items: View invoices generated from validated timesheets and services.
Track statuses (draft, issued, paid) and amounts per client.
Open an invoice to see line details and history.

missions.title: What is this module for?
items: Reference missions and link them to a client.
Assign collaborators to each mission for timesheets and budgets.
Create a new mission from the button at top right.

prestations.title: What is this module for?
items: Validate service timesheets before invoicing or export.
Filter and sort services by status, client or period.
Export the validated batch as XML for external integration.

dashboard.title: What is this module for?
items: Overview: KPIs for active modules, pending validations, alerts.
Quick access to modules via cards and shortcuts.
AI briefing summarises recent activity when the module is enabled.

howto.title: How it works
howto.item_code: The code identifies the rule (unique): use a stable slug (e.g. "leave-requested").
howto.item_trigger: The trigger is a business event name (e.g. "tma.demand.created").
howto.item_frequency: Frequency defines when notifications are sent (immediate or batched).
howto.item_template: Template is the message content that will be sent (text).

tooltip.trigger: Event name that triggers the send.
tooltip.frequency: When to send: immediate or scheduled.
tooltip.attach_pdf: Adds a PDF version as an attachment (if available).

hint.code: Examples: "leave-requested", "tma-demand-created". Avoid spaces and uppercase letters.
hint.trigger: Example: "tma.demand.created". Must match a trigger supported by the API.
hint.frequency: Immediate sends right away. Other options batch and send at a fixed time.
hint.template: Write the message as it should appear in the notification.
hint.attach_pdf: Enables adding a PDF attachment when sending.

landing.args_subtitle: Margin pressure, mandatory e-invoicing, fragmented tools.
landing.args_1_desc: Validated timesheet → client PDF → invoice. One data, one flow.
landing.args_2_desc: UO and TMA in real time. Alerts before overrun.
landing.args_3_desc: EN 16931 e-invoicing on the 2026 roadmap. Not a side project.
landing.modules_subtitle: 6 MVP modules. Add as you grow.
landing.pricing_teaser_subtitle: Premium per-seat bundles — from timesheets to full ESN rollout.
landing.faq_1_a: No. Timesheets first, the rest when you're ready.
landing.faq_2_a: EN 16931 is on the product roadmap (billing module), not yet shipped in MVP.
landing.faq_3_a: A few days after a 30-minute scoping call.
landing.mid_cta_text: See the real product. No empty promises.
landing.diagram_architecture_subtitle: One data. One branding. One compliance.
landing.diagram_pillars_alt: Kore pillars: TMA, timesheets, UO budget

pricing.title: Pricing
pricing.subtitle: 3 premium per-seat editions — pick the scope that fits your consultancy
pricing.note: Public list prices ex. VAT. Monthly or annual billing. À la carte modules available on request for Enterprise.
pricing.cta_text: Book a 30-min demo to size the edition and seat count.

starter.title: Starter
starter.desc: Time & attendance for SMBs and growing teams.

enterprise.title: Enterprise
enterprise.desc: Full suite, compliance and advanced integrations.

modules_page.title: Kore modules
modules_page.subtitle: Modular suite — enable only what you need

ai.disclaimer: Review content before saving. AI may be wrong.
ai.disabled_tenant: AI assistance is not enabled for your organisation.
ai.cra_prefill_result: {n} suggestion(s) available — apply manually in the grid.
ai.chat_disclosure: You are interacting with a Kore AI assistant.

ia.notice_intro: Kore AI assistance helps with drafting and analysis. It does not replace human validation.
ia.notice_capabilities: Capabilities: TMA drafts, CRA suggestions, dashboard briefing, etc.
ia.notice_limits: Limits: risk of errors; outputs are not decision-grade without review.
ia.notice_duties: Deployer duties (EU AI Act Art. 26): inform workers and supervise usage.
ia.deployer_intro: By enabling AI, you act as deployer under the EU AI Act.
ia.deployer_supervision: Supervise usage: suggestions remain non-decisional without human validation.
ia.deployer_transparency: Transparency: inform staff about enabled capabilities and limits.
ia.deployer_logging: Traceability: AI requests are logged (capability, model, context).
ia.accept_notice: I have read and accept the AI usage notice
ia.workers_informed: I confirm workers have been (or will be) informed
ia.enabled_info: AI assistance is active. Collaborators see « AI generated » badges on relevant content.

conges.reset_desc: Default labels for country {country} will overwrite existing entries (same codes). Custom types you added manually are kept if they are not in the country catalogue.

cra.week_start_hint: Used to split weeks in monthly timesheets for the tenant.

demandes.channels_hint: At least one channel must stay enabled. Disabling hides the menu and blocks new items; history remains readable.
demandes.guides_enabled: Show help blocks on request pages
demandes.guides_hint: Users can also hide help individually.

securite.intro: Configure two-factor authentication (TOTP) for company users.
securite.totp_default_warning: Users without 2FA will need to set up an authenticator app on next sign-in.
securite.totp_user_configurable_hint: When 2FA is not enabled by default, users can still opt in from their profile.
